description: SDK round 2: Add stripUndefined utility, widen buildQueryString type to accept mixed arrays, add parseRateLimitHeader for non-standard rate limit headers, update CLAUDE.md, then bump version to 0.0.71 and publish to npm.
qualityChecks: npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run test

userStories.title: Add stripUndefined utility to fetch.ts
userStories.description: Add a stripUndefined function that filters out keys with undefined values from an object. This is the most commonly repeated pattern across all plugins — 50+ tool files manually build request bodies with if (x !== undefined) body.x = x chains.
acceptanceCriteria: stripUndefined<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T): Partial<T> filters out undefined values
Keeps null, 0, false, empty string — only removes undefined
Exported from fetch.ts and re-exported from index.ts
Unit test in fetch.test.ts covers: filters undefined, keeps null, keeps falsy values, returns empty object for all-undefined input

userStories.title: Widen buildQueryString type to accept mixed arrays
userStories.description: Change buildQueryString's type signature to accept (string | number | boolean)[] instead of just string[]. Sentry, Vercel, and Cloudflare can't adopt buildQueryString because their query parameters include number[] and boolean[] arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: buildQueryString accepts Record<string, string | number | boolean | (string | number | boolean)[] | undefined>
Array items are converted to strings via String() before appending
Existing behavior unchanged for string[] arrays
Unit test covers number[] and boolean[] array values
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts line 62

Current signature:
```typescript
export const buildQueryString = (params: Record<string, string | number | boolean | string[] | undefined>): string => {
```

New signature:
```typescript
export const buildQueryString = (
  params: Record<string, string | number | boolean | (string | number | boolean)[] | undefined>,
): string => {
```

The implementation already uses `String(value)` for non-array items and iterates arrays with `.append(key, item)`. The only change needed is:
1. Widen the type signature
2. In the array branch, convert each item to string: `searchParams.append(key, String(item))` instead of `searchParams.append(key, item)`

The current array branch just does `searchParams.append(key, item)` where `item` is typed as `string`. After widening, `item` becomes `string | number | boolean`, so wrap with `String(item)`.

Constraints:
- This is a backwards-compatible type widening — no breaking changes
- Do NOT change the function's runtime behavior for existing input types

userStories.title: Add parseRateLimitHeader utility to fetch.ts
userStories.description: Add a parseRateLimitHeader function that checks multiple common rate limit header names and normalizes them all to retryAfterMs. X uses x-rate-limit-reset (epoch), Reddit uses x-ratelimit-reset (seconds until reset), while standard APIs use Retry-After.
acceptanceCriteria: parseRateLimitHeader(headers: Headers): number | undefined
Checks headers in order: Retry-After, x-rate-limit-reset, x-ratelimit-reset, RateLimit-Reset
Retry-After is parsed via existing parseRetryAfterMs (handles both seconds and HTTP-date)
x-rate-limit-reset (epoch seconds) is converted: value * 1000 - Date.now()
x-ratelimit-reset (seconds until reset) is converted: value * 1000
Returns undefined if no rate limit header is found or value is invalid
Exported from fetch.ts and re-exported from index.ts

userStories.title: Update plugin-sdk CLAUDE.md with new utilities
userStories.description: Update platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md to document stripUndefined, the widened buildQueryString type, and parseRateLimitHeader.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch section includes stripUndefined with signature and one-line description
Fetch section's buildQueryString description reflects the widened array type
Fetch section includes parseRateLimitHeader with signature and description
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md

Add to the Fetch Utilities section:
- `stripUndefined<T>(obj)` → `Partial<T>` — filters out keys with undefined values, keeps null/0/false/empty string
- Update `buildQueryString` description to mention `(string | number | boolean)[]` array support
- `parseRateLimitHeader(headers)` → `number | undefined` — checks Retry-After, x-rate-limit-reset, x-ratelimit-reset, RateLimit-Reset and normalizes to milliseconds

Constraints:
- Follow the existing one-line-per-function documentation style
- Do NOT change any other sections

userStories.title: Bump version to 0.0.71 and publish to npm
userStories.description: Load the bump-version skill and execute every step: bump all version references, build, publish all 7 platform packages to npm in dependency order, rebuild all plugins to update lock files, and commit everything.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 platform package.json files have version 0.0.71
Chrome extension manifest.json has version 0.0.71
All plugin package.json files have version 0.0.71 with SDK/tools dep ranges ^0.0.71
All 7 platform packages are published to npm at 0.0.71 (verify with npm view)
All plugin package-lock.json files reference the published 0.0.71 versions
No hardcoded 0.0.70 version strings remain in platform/ TypeScript source
userStories.notes:
Use the `skill` tool to load the `bump-version` skill, then follow every step in the 'Reference: What the Worker Does' section exactly.

The skill is at `.claude/skills/bump-version/__SKILL__.md` — read it first.

Key steps in order:
1. Update version in all 7 platform package.json files: shared, plugin-sdk, browser-extension, mcp-server, plugin-tools, cli, create-plugin
2. Update version in platform/browser-extension/manifest.json
3. Update version AND dependency ranges in all plugin package.json files (plugins/*/package.json)
4. Run `npm install --package-lock-only` at repo root to update root lock file
5. Scan for hardcoded '0.0.70' strings in platform/**/*.ts
6. Run `npm run build` and `npm run type-check`
7. Commit the version bump (message: 'bump version to 0.0.71')
8. Verify npm auth: `npm whoami` must return 'REDACTED_NPM_USER'
9. Publish in this exact order:
   - cd platform/shared && npm publish
   - cd platform/browser-extension && npm publish
   - cd platform/mcp-server && npm publish
   - cd platform/plugin-sdk && npm publish
   - cd platform/plugin-tools && npm publish
   - cd platform/cli && npm publish
   - cd platform/create-plugin && npm publish
10. After all 7 are published, verify: `npm view @opentabs-dev/shared@0.0.71 version`
11. Run `npm run build:plugins` to install published versions and rebuild all plugins
12. Commit plugin lock file updates (message: 'update plugin lock files for 0.0.71')

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any publishConfig.access values
- Do NOT bump the docs/ version
- Do NOT modify the root package.json
- Plugin packages use `^0.0.71` semver ranges, NOT exact versions
- The publish MUST happen before build:plugins
